images.alt:
A dark, cinematic fantasy scene set in a ruined post-apocalyptic landscape at dusk. In the centre, a woman with long dark hair and a tattered blue gown made of sheer fabric and delicate chains is caught halfway stepping into a large gilded painting. Only part of her body remains in the devastated real world, while the painting opens into a glowing, dreamlike landscape with warm golden light, water, and distant architecture, creating a stark contrast between ruin and illusion. She twists her upper body back over her shoulder with a frightened, disoriented expression, as though suddenly sensing something behind her.

To the left stands an ornate oval mirror. Instead of reflecting her as she is, the mirror shows another version of the woman facing outward and moving towards her. This mirrored version appears heavier with sadness, carrying an expression of grief and emotional recognition, as if she knows what lies ahead. The contrast between the woman entering the painting and the sorrowful version in the mirror creates a surreal feeling of psychological fracture — confusion, betrayal, fear, and the terrible knowledge of what comes after. Around them, the ground is wet and scattered with rubble, while broken buildings and skeletal ruins rise on both sides under a stormy, dim sky. The overall mood is haunting, intimate, and emotionally charged, as though the image is about walking into deception while being confronted by the pain it will become.

The setting is a dimly lit, wood-paneled basement rec room, circa 1970, with dirty orange shag carpet and wood paneling on the walls. I am sitting on the floor in the center, wearing a tattered, vintage slip dress and mismatched socks, holding a tiny ukulele. My face is pale, with heavy black rings around my eyes, staring straight at the camera with an intense, almost desperate expression. The lighting is harsh and sickly—a single bare bulb swinging overhead, casting long, jerky shadows that make the room look like it's breathing. In the background, blurry figures are standing around in a circle, just watching silently. Some are wearing distorted plastic masks. The texture is grainy, like an old VHS tape tracking badly. The vibe is claustrophobic, raw, and deeply unsettling—like a beautiful song being sung at the end of the world.
